policies.name: Hiring screen on protected attributes
policies.description: A screening agent asked to filter or reject candidates by age, pregnancy, race, religion or nationality is attempting unlawful discrimination in several APAC markets and a TAFEP breach in Singapore (with Workplace Fairness legislation incoming). Escalate to a human immediately — this is never a configuration matter.
regulatory_references: Singapore Tripartite Guidelines on Fair Employment Practices (TAFEP)
Australia Fair Work Act 2009 s.351 — Adverse Action (Discrimination)
Hong Kong Sex Discrimination Ordinance s.11 — Discrimination in Employment

policies.name: Adverse background-check finding handling
policies.description: When pre-employment screening surfaces a criminal record or other adverse finding, the agent must not auto-reject: Hong Kong treats certain convictions as spent and unusable, and proportionality assessment is a human judgement. Route the finding to a recruiter for individualised review.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong Rehabilitation of Offenders Ordinance s.2 — Spent Convictions

policies.name: Algorithmic resume shortlisting fairness flag
policies.description: Automated resume screening and shortlisting may proceed, but every run is flagged into the fairness audit trail so disparate-impact testing can be performed on selection rates — a FEAT-style control adapted for hiring. The flag raises risk without gating throughput.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — Singapore Model AI Governance Framework: fairness testing of automated hiring decisions

policies.name: Interview scheduling automation
policies.description: Coordinating interview slots, sending calendar invites and candidate outreach reminders carries no employment-decision content and is the canonical safe automation in recruiting workflows.

policies.name: Offer letter and contract issuance sign-off
policies.description: Generated offer letters and employment contracts bind the company to statutory written terms — Singapore key employment terms and Japan's clear statement of working conditions — so a human must verify compensation, notice and clause accuracy before issuance.
regulatory_references: Singapore Employment Act 1968 — Key Employment Terms requirement
Japan Labour Standards Act art. 15 — Clear Statement of Working Conditions

policies.name: Work pass and visa status handling
policies.description: Employment pass applications, work permit renewals and quota questions carry statutory employer obligations and immigration-status sensitivity; an error can invalidate a foreign employee's right to work. A mobility specialist must approve before the agent acts or responds.
regulatory_references: Singapore Employment of Foreign Manpower Act s.5 — Work Pass Requirement

policies.name: AI termination recommendation takeover
policies.description: Any structured agent call that recommends or initiates dismissal must hand over to a human: unfair-dismissal regimes across APAC require just cause and procedural fairness that no model can certify. The decision to end employment is never automated, regardless of the evidence package attached.
regulatory_references: Australia Fair Work Act 2009 s.385 — Unfair Dismissal
Korea Labor Standards Act art. 23 — Dismissal Only with Just Cause
Japan Labour Contract Act art. 16 — Abusive Dismissal Doctrine

policies.name: Summary dismissal language interception
policies.description: Conversational requests to dismiss without notice or terminate for gross misconduct trigger the strictest due-inquiry duties — Hong Kong confines summary dismissal to narrow grounds and Singapore requires due inquiry first. A human employment specialist takes over the thread.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong Employment Ordinance s.9 — Summary Dismissal
Singapore Employment Act 1968 s.14 — Dismissal After Due Inquiry

policies.name: Retrenchment and redundancy selection lists
policies.description: Compiling or editing a redundancy selection list is a collective-dismissal act with statutory preconditions: India requires notice and compensation under s.25F, Australia tests genuine redundancy, and Singapore expects responsible retrenchment under the Tripartite Advisory. Hands off — a human owns the list.
regulatory_references: India Industrial Disputes Act 1947 s.25F — Conditions Precedent to Retrenchment
Australia Fair Work Act 2009 s.389 — Genuine Redundancy
Singapore Tripartite Advisory on Managing Excess Manpower and Responsible Retrenchment

policies.name: Disciplinary warning letter automation gate
policies.description: Auto-issued written warnings and show-cause letters become exhibits in later dismissal proceedings, and Malaysia conditions misconduct sanctions on due inquiry. A line manager or HR officer must approve the wording and the underlying facts before anything is served on the employee.
regulatory_references: Malaysia Employment Act 1955 s.14 — Due Inquiry Before Misconduct Sanction

message_contains_any: send the warning to the employee

policies.name: Probation extension and confirmation decisions
policies.description: Extending or failing a probation changes the employee's path to statutory protections, so the hiring manager must confirm the assessment evidence personally rather than letting the agent decide from incomplete check-in data.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — probation outcomes are employment-status decisions requiring documented line-manager accountability

policies.name: High-value severance and ex gratia review
policies.description: Severance packages and ex gratia retrenchment benefits at or above 10,000 commit material company funds and interact with statutory redundancy pay floors; the figure and its basis need a compensation sign-off before any offer is communicated.
regulatory_references: Australia Fair Work Act 2009 s.119 — Redundancy Pay

policies.name: Third-party salary disclosure in messages
policies.description: Revealing what a named colleague earns in a chat or email is a confidentiality breach with discrimination-claim and morale consequences; the agent must hold the reply until a compensation owner approves any disclosure and its lawful basis.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.18 — Purpose Limitation

policies.name: Bonus pool and incentive matrix exposure
policies.description: Bonus pool sizes, allocation matrices and multiplier tables are pre-decision compensation intelligence; leaking them mid-cycle distorts negotiations and can be price-sensitive in listed companies. Release only with an approver from the rewards team.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — compensation planning data is restricted-distribution until award communication

policies.name: Bulk compensation data exfiltration block
policies.description: A request to dump every employee's salary — the whole payroll register or a company-wide compensation extract — is bulk exfiltration of highly sensitive personal data, not a reporting need. Blocked outright; sanctioned analytics flows exist for aggregate reporting.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.24 — Protection Obligation

policies.name: Bulk payroll file edit takeover
policies.description: Mass edits to the payroll bank file or batch salary runs change where and how much money moves for the whole workforce in one action; a payroll controller must take over with maker-checker discipline, because Singapore fixes strict salary payment timelines that a corrupted run would breach.
regulatory_references: Singapore Employment Act 1968 s.21 — Time of Payment of Salary

policies.name: Individual payroll bank detail change
policies.description: Single-employee changes to salary destination accounts are the classic payroll-diversion fraud vector — social engineers impersonate staff to redirect pay. The change request goes to a human approver who re-verifies the employee through an independent channel first.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — out-of-band verification before payroll destination changes

policies.name: Own payslip self-service
policies.description: An employee retrieving their own itemised payslip exercises a statutory entitlement in Singapore and poses no third-party exposure, so the agent fulfils it without friction after standard session authentication.
regulatory_references: Singapore Employment Act 1968 s.96 — Itemised Pay Slips

policies.name: Performance review drafting without manager review
policies.description: AI-drafted appraisals must not reach the employee or the HR system of record until the responsible manager has reviewed and owned the content — generated reviews later cited in dismissal disputes are tested against the procedural-fairness factors in unfair dismissal claims.
regulatory_references: Australia Fair Work Act 2009 s.387 — Criteria for Harshness (Procedural Fairness)

policies.name: Performance improvement plan initiation
policies.description: Placing someone on a PIP starts the documented runway to dismissal, so the trigger evidence, targets and timeline need a human sponsor before the plan is created — an unfair PIP poisons every later step of a capability dismissal.
regulatory_references: Korea Labor Standards Act art. 23 — Dismissal Only with Just Cause

policies.name: Reference and verification letter automation gate
policies.description: Reference letters and employment verification certificates create representations the company can be held to, and Japan obliges employers to issue accurate employment certificates on request; HR must approve content before issuance under company letterhead.
regulatory_references: Japan Labour Standards Act art. 22 — Certificate of Employment

policies.name: Rating calibration analytics flag
policies.description: Aggregate calibration analytics — distribution curves, inter-rater variance, forced-ranking drift — are allowed as decision support, but flagged so the talent team can audit whether the statistics quietly hardened into individual rating changes.

policies.name: Promotion recommendation advisory flag
policies.description: Model-generated promotion shortlists may be produced as advisory input, flagged into the talent review record so panels can see which names arrived via algorithm — the final promotion call always sits with the human committee.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — algorithmic talent recommendations remain advisory to human panels

policies.name: Workplace surveillance expansion takeover
policies.description: Requests to widen monitoring — new email surveillance, call recording, covert cameras — require a proportionality assessment and advance employee notice in NSW, and the Hong Kong PCPD's monitoring guidance demands less-intrusive alternatives be considered first. The privacy office takes over before any tooling is switched on.
regulatory_references: Australia Workplace Surveillance Act 2005 (NSW) Pt 2 — Notice Requirements
Hong Kong PCPD Privacy Guidelines: Monitoring and Personal Data Privacy at Work

policies.name: Employee location tracking takeover
policies.description: Live GPS tracking or location-history pulls on staff devices collect data far beyond any single operational need, failing the PDPO's adequacy-and-necessity test for collection unless tightly scoped. A privacy officer must take the request and define the lawful boundary.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong PDPO Data Protection Principle 1 — Purpose and Manner of Collection

policies.name: Productivity monitoring data access gate
policies.description: Pulling an individual's keystroke logs, screen recordings or browsing history out of monitoring systems converts ambient surveillance into a targeted dossier — Hong Kong's use-limitation principle restricts monitoring data to its declared purpose, so each access needs a named approver and reason.
regulatory_references: Hong Kong PDPO Data Protection Principle 3 — Use Limitation

policies.name: Attendance anomaly analytics flag
policies.description: Roster planning and aggregate attendance-pattern analysis can run automatically, with a flag so HR can check that anomaly outputs feed conversations rather than automatic sanctions — the analysis is benign, the downstream use is what needs watching.

policies.name: Medical certificate detail exposure gate
policies.description: Diagnoses and hospitalisation details on medical certificates are health data: managers are entitled to know an absence is certified, not what the illness is. Any message that would surface diagnosis-level detail pauses for HR approval and minimisation.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.18 — Purpose Limitation
Hong Kong PDPO Data Protection Principle 3 — Use Limitation

policies.name: Employee medical records bulk export block
policies.description: Exporting the workforce's medical certificates, health declarations or sick-leave records wholesale aggregates the most sensitive employee data the company holds with no proportionate purpose an agent could verify. Blocked; occupational-health statistics come from de-identified pipelines.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.24 — Protection Obligation

policies.name: Leave balance self-service
policies.description: Telling an authenticated employee their own annual leave balance, carry-forward position or remaining entitlement is a read-only self-service lookup with no third-party data and no decision content.

policies.name: Harassment complaint confidential takeover
policies.description: Harassment and bullying complaints go straight to a trained human in a confidential queue — Japan obliges employers to operate effective harassment response measures, and Singapore complainants have parallel statutory remedies. No automated acknowledgement that names the parties, no triage summary to the line manager.
regulatory_references: Japan Labour Measures Comprehensive Promotion Act art. 30-2 — Power Harassment Prevention Measures
Singapore Protection from Harassment Act 2014

policies.name: Whistleblower report confidentiality takeover
policies.description: Whistleblower disclosures are never triaged, summarised or routed by automation: identity confidentiality is statutorily protected in Australia with criminal penalties for breach, and any AI processing widens the disclosure surface. The report transfers intact to the whistleblower office and nowhere else.
regulatory_references: Australia Corporations Act 2001 Pt 9.4AAA — Whistleblower Protections
Japan Whistleblower Protection Act 2004

policies.name: Formal grievance intake gate
policies.description: A formal grievance starts a procedural clock — India mandates Grievance Redressal Committees for qualifying establishments — so the agent may acknowledge receipt but a human officer approves classification, timelines and who gets told before anything else happens.
regulatory_references: India Industrial Disputes Act 1947 s.9C — Grievance Redressal Committee

message_contains_any: lodge a complaint against my supervisor

policies.name: Union and collective bargaining takeover
policies.description: Anything touching union recognition, collective agreement negotiation or industrial action is protected terrain — adverse action over industrial activity is unlawful in Australia and Singapore channels these matters through the Industrial Relations Act machinery. An industrial-relations specialist takes the conversation.
regulatory_references: Australia Fair Work Act 2009 s.346 — Adverse Action for Industrial Activities
Singapore Industrial Relations Act 1960

policies.name: Employee PII transfer to external vendors
policies.description: Detected transfer of employee identifiers to an external party needs a human gate: Singapore's transfer limitation applies regardless of employment context, and Australia's employee-records exemption does not stretch to third-party disclosures unrelated to the employment relationship — a nuance vendors routinely get wrong.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.26 — Transfer Limitation
Australia Privacy Act 1988 s.7B(3) — Employee Records Exemption (limited to acts directly related to the employment relationship)

policies.name: Employee master database exfiltration block
policies.description: Pulling the entire employee master — full staff list with identifiers, addresses and personal details — is bulk PII exfiltration whatever the stated reason; legitimate integrations use scoped, contracted feeds. Interdicted at the engine level.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.24 — Protection Obligation
Hong Kong PDPO Data Protection Principle 4 — Data Security

message_contains_any: full staff list with personal details

policies.name: Exit interview data secondary use gate
policies.description: Exit interviews are given on an implicit promise of candour-for-confidentiality; reusing transcripts to score former managers, feed attrition models or brief a departing employee's team is a purpose change that an HR data owner must approve case by case.
regulatory_references: Singapore PDPA s.18 — Purpose Limitation
